fundamental_rights: Article 21 — Right to Privacy (recognised by Supreme Court in Puttaswamy judgment 2017)
Article 19(1)(a) — Right to Freedom of Speech (includes protection from online harassment)
Article 14 — Right to Equality before Law

laws.name: Information Technology Act, 2000
sections: Section 66C (identity theft — 3 years + Rs 1 lakh fine)
Section 66E (violation of privacy — publishing private images)
laws.summary: Primary cyber law. Covers hacking, identity theft, online fraud, and privacy violations.
laws.remedy: File FIR at Cyber Crime Police Station or report at cybercrime.gov.in

laws.name: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023
laws.summary: Criminal provisions for online fraud, extortion, stalking, and cheating.
laws.remedy: File FIR at local police station

laws.name: Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007
laws.summary: Governs UPI, NEFT, RTGS fraud. RBI directs banks to resolve disputes.
laws.remedy: Report to bank immediately, then RBI Banking Ombudsman

laws.name: Personal Data Protection Bill / IT (Intermediary Guidelines) Rules 2021
laws.summary: Platforms must remove unlawful content within 36 hours of notice.
laws.remedy: Report to platform's Grievance Officer and NCW if involving women

steps: IMMEDIATELY: Change all passwords and enable two-factor authentication
For financial fraud: call your bank's 24x7 helpline within 3 hours — report on cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930
Take screenshots and preserve all evidence (chats, emails, transaction IDs) before they disappear
File a complaint at cybercrime.gov.in (National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal) — available 24/7
Alternatively, file FIR at the nearest Cyber Crime Police Station
For social media abuse: report to the platform AND file complaint at NCRP portal
If financial loss occurred: also file complaint with your bank's fraud department and RBI Ombudsman
